title: Bulk report - Week 21
summary:
Capesize
The Capesize market ended the week on a firmer note after a shaky start, recovering well from early pressure caused by uncertainty in Guinea. The announcement of revoked mining licenses initially rattled sentiment, but the market quickly regained composure. The Pacific basin emerged as the strongest performer, underpinned by consistent miner and operator demand.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The Capesize market ended the week on a firmer note after a shaky start, recovering well from early pressure caused by uncertainty in Guinea. The announcement of revoked mining licenses initially rattled sentiment, but the market quickly regained composure.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: Another softer week for the Panamax market as owners felt the recent pressure continue across all basins. Owners' resistance was hard to find with early tonnage and ballaster tonnage continuing to discount.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: The week started off certainly from the Atlantic on a slightly more positive note, but as the week closed some of this sentiment had eroded slightly. Support was seen from the US Gulf, a 63,000-dwt fixing a trip from Houston to India with petcoke at $19,750.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: It was generally a positive week, with freight rates increasing across most loading areas. The Continent-Mediterranean region continued to show steady improvement, with market sentiment remaining largely positional.
